//: Static site. No build AND no install runs on Vercel, deliberately.
committed and verified, so there is nothing for Vercel to compile or
1. With outputDirectory '.', a Vercel build would upload the whole
2. devDependencies include sharp, a native binary. Nothing in the deploy
needs it, and it is a pointless failure surface on every deploy.
3. An empty installCommand ('') makes Vercel run its DEFAULT (npm
install) — which is what surfaced the lenis/tempus deprecation
warning. A no-op command ('echo …') makes it truly skip install.
outputDirectory MUST stay declared. Without it Vercel defaults to looking
for a public/ directory, doesn't find one, and fails the whole deploy.
To rebuild the artefacts: npm install && npm run build (locally), commit.
buildCommand: echo skip: prebuilt static artefacts are committed
